completed_work: Saved COMP-0011 warning admission cap default comparison.
Saved SYNCOMP-0007 synthesis of the default decision.
key_decisions: Default max_warning_admissions to 1 only when provider latest-context uses require_admission=True and no cap is explicitly set.
Preserve explicit override precedence: 0, 1, 2, etc. must all pass through unchanged.
Keep build_context_pack.py explicit for now unless later intentionally included; this default is scoped to provider latest-context runs.
next_actions: Implement shared provider warning-cap default resolver and wire it into ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py.
risks: Silent behavior changes are possible if the resolver is applied too broadly; centralize it and test scope carefully.
summary_text: AI-Lab ran COMP-0011 and SYNCOMP-0007 to decide whether max_warning_admissions=1 should become the default. The synthesis chose a shared policy-resolution helper: when a provider latest-context command has require_admission=True and max_warning_admissions is unset, resolve the cap to 1. Explicit values still win: 0 remains strict, values greater than 1 remain permissive, and unset with require_admission=False remains uncapped. The manifest already records admission_policy and admission_summary, so the resolved policy will remain auditable. Scope is provider latest-context runs only; do not change warning definitions, detectors, admission criteria, or historical manifests. Next implementation should centralize the resolver and wire ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py to use it.
